'''Elie El-Zakhem''' is a [[Lebanon national rugby league team|Lebanon]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Western Suburbs Magpies]] in the [[NSW Cup]].

He has previously played for the [[Canterbury Bulldogs]], the [[Parramatta Eels]], the [[Sydney Roosters]] and the [[North Sydney Bears]] in the [[NSW Cup]].

==Club career==
===North Sydney Bears===
On 24 September 2023, El-Zakhem played for North Sydney in their 2023 NSW Cup grand final loss against [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-blake-taaffe-zach-dockarclay-dean-hawkins-south-sydney-rabbitohs-north-sydney-bears-nsw-cup-grand-final-stats-result/news-story/befffd38099aec69314f2ff2171b9376/|title=Taaffe's statement as Souths end 40-year drought; veteran's captain's knock: Reserves Wrap|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=25 September 2023 }}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers===
On 29 September 2023, the [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of El-Zakhem on a two-year deal from the [[2024 Super League season]].<ref name="CT Signing">{{Cite web |date=29 September 2023 |title=El-Zakhem is a Tiger |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8778 |access-date=22 October 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref name="BBC Putt El-Zakhem Westerman">{{Cite web |date=29 September 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers: Putt and El-Zakhem join, new contract for Joe Westerman |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66958025 |access-date=22 October 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He would be joining his Lebanon teammate [[Charbel Tasipale]] at the Tigers upon recommendation from former Castleford player [[Jordan Rankin]].<ref name="YEP Cas Signing">{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=29 September 2023 |title=Ex-player helps out as Castleford Tigers sign Lebanon World Cup star |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/ex-player-helps-out-as-castleford-tigers-sign-lebanon-world-cup-star-4354504 |access-date=22 October 2023 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}</ref>

El-Zakhem was assigned squad number 11 ahead of the [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=20 January 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers 2024 squad numbers: 11 new signings receive their jerseys, with 31 shirts handed out |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-2024-squad-numbers-11-new-signings-receive-their-jerseys-with-31-shirts-handed-out |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He made his [[Super League]] debut for Castleford in round 1 against the [[Wigan Warriors]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Freeman |first=Jay |date=17 February 2024 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 4-32 Wigan Warriors - Reigning champions begin with hard-earned win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68304778 |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

===Mackay Cutters===
On 23 November 2024 it was reported that he would join [[Mackay Cutters]] in the [[Queensland Cup]] for the 2025 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=23 November 2024|title=Ex-Castleford Tigers forward Elie El-Zakhem's new club now confirmed|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-castleford-tigers-forward-elie-el-zakhems-new-club-now-confirmed/|author=Callum Walker}}</ref> He underwent pre-season training with the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] before returning to Sydney to join the [[Western Suburbs Magpies]] in the [[NSW Cup]].<ref>{{cite web | title=Gallery: Cowboys return for day one of pre-season | date=19 November 2024 | url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2024/11/19/gallery-first-day-back/ }}</ref>

== International career ==
El-Zakhem made his international debut for [[Lebanon national rugby league team|Lebanon]] in their 56-14 loss to [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] in the 2019 Pacific Test.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/fiji-v-lebanon-international-2019/719011#lineup |title=Fiji vs Lebanon |publisher=Wide World of Sports |date=22 June 2019}}</ref>

In September 2022, El-Zakhem was named in the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup squads#Lebanon|Lebanon squad]] for the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup|2021 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walter |first=Brad |date=29 September 2023 |title=Moses named in Lebanon World Cup squad but Cheika sweating on Kangaroos call |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2022/09/29/moses-named-in-lebanon-world-cup-squad-but-cheika-sweating-on-kangaroos-call/ |access-date=22 October 2023 |website=NRL.com}}</ref> He featured in every game for the Cedars, making 4 appearances and scoring 2 tries.<ref name="RLRKC" />
